name: cAMP-PKA signaling activated Rpn6-phosphorylated mammalian proteasomes
summary: Mammalian-cell evidence that proteasome activity can be acutely enhanced by cAMP/PKA-driven Rpn6 phosphorylation.
statement: In mammalian cell lines and purified 26S proteasomes, cAMP/PKA signaling phosphorylated Rpn6/PSMD11 at Ser14, increased proteasome activity, and promoted degradation of short-lived and aggregation-prone proteins.
population_or_model: Mammalian cell lines, purified 26S proteasomes, and aggregation-prone protein substrates
quantitative_note: The source reports enhanced hydrolysis of ubiquitinated proteins, small peptides, and ATP, plus increased doubly capped 26S proteasomes after cAMP-raising treatments.
caveats: This is mechanistic cell and biochemical evidence, not organismal aging evidence.
cAMP/PKA activation has many non-proteasome effects.
The result supports tractability of proteasome activation but not clinical benefit.
